mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents through standardized communication protocols. It simplifies the deployment complexity and enhances security for agent-based applications, making it suitable for production environments. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes environment and potential overhead in managing the infrastructure.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the Kagenti platform.

```bash

curl -Lo kagenti-cli https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64

chmod +x kagenti-cli

sudo mv kagenti-cli /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Kagenti CLI binary.
> Make the CLI executable.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.30

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> Fetch the cluster credentials.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in the Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes and their statuses.

steps.title: Install Kagenti on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti platform on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Add the Kagenti Helm repository.
> Install Kagenti in the 'kagenti' namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti installation from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> Uninstall the Kagenti release.
> Delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your configurations and data. This step will upgrade Kagenti to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Backup current configurations.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes cluster unreachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig may not be set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --zone=us-central1-a' to update kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install chart: ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm chart version may not be compatible with the current Kubernetes version.

**Fix:**
Check compatibility and use 'helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version <compatible-version>' to install.

**Affected versions:** <= 1.29

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be misconfigurations in the deployment or insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust resource requests/limits as necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the Kagenti deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in the cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30, improved security features, and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kagenti' showing all pods in 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
